rationale: The citing paper uses Iyer and Villas-Boas (2003) to justify a core modeling choice: focusing on linear wholesale pricing rather than nonlinear two-part tariffs. In the main text and footnote discussion, it cites this paper as evidence that two-part tariffs are limited in practice and as one explanation for why such contracts may be less prevalent. This is more than a passing mention because it supports the paper's conceptual setup and boundary conditions, even though the citing paper does not directly extend the cited paper's bargaining model.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Iyer and Villas-Boas (2003) as empirical/theoretical support that simple linear contracts are common and two-part tariffs are limited, which motivates its baseline contract assumptions and interpretation of contractual incompleteness.
